howItWorks.intro: This filament cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Cost per Gram = Spool Price ÷ Spool Weight
Print Cost = Cost per Gram × Print Weight
Adjusted Cost = Print Cost × (1 + Fail Rate / 100)
Prints per Spool = Spool Weight ÷ Print Weight (rounded down)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much does 3D printing filament cost per gram?
faq.answer: Most PLA filament costs $0.02-$0.03 per gram ($20-$30 per 1kg spool). Specialty filaments like carbon fiber or flexible TPU can cost $0.04-$0.10 per gram.

faq.question: How do I estimate the weight of my 3D print?
faq.answer: Most slicing software (Cura, PrusaSlicer) shows estimated print weight. As a rough guide, a solid 1cm cube of PLA weighs about 1.24 grams.

faq.question: Should I account for failed prints in my cost estimate?
faq.answer: Yes. Beginners typically have a 10-20% fail rate, while experienced users average 2-5%. Factor this into cost calculations for accurate budgeting.

faq.question: Is the Filament Cost Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard 3D printing and additive manufacturing formulas trusted by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: How much does PLA filament cost per gram?
faq.answer: Standard PLA filament costs $0.02-$0.03 per gram based on typical 1kg spool prices of $20-$30. Premium or specialty PLA (silk, matte, wood-fill) runs $0.03-$0.05 per gram. Buying in bulk or during sales can drop costs below $0.02/gram.

faq.question: How much filament does a typical 3D print use?
faq.answer: Small prints (keychains, small figurines) use 5-20 grams. Medium prints (phone cases, brackets) use 30-80 grams. Large prints (vases, cosplay parts) can use 200-500+ grams. Your slicer software shows the exact weight estimate before printing.

faq.question: Which 3D printing filament is cheapest?
faq.answer: PLA is the cheapest common filament at $20-$30 per kg. PETG runs $22-$35/kg. ABS costs $20-$30/kg but requires an enclosure. TPU (flexible) is $25-$40/kg. Specialty filaments like carbon fiber, nylon, and polycarbonate range from $35-$80/kg.

sections.title: What Is the Filament Cost Calculator?
sections.body: The Filament Cost Calculator is a free online tool designed for makers and 3D printing enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the 3D printing and additive manufacturing space. By entering your spool price, spool weight, print weight, you get instant results including cost per gram, print cost, adjusted cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cost per gram right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In 3D printing and additive manufacturing, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your prints with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your spool price and need to find the right cost per gram. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Spool Price ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your spool price — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: spool weight, print weight, expected fail rate. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your cost per gram, print cost, adjusted cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your spool price before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both cost per gram and print cost — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your prints.
If you're unsure about your expected fail rate, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
